steps.title: Identify the clause types
steps.content:
The text before the blank is an independent clause: “Since 1989, Indianapolis's Eiteljorg Museum has celebrated the work of groundbreaking contemporary Native.”
The text after the blank is also an independent clause: “in 2013, it awarded Buffalohead one of its prestigious artist fellowships.”

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction, making choice D the correct punctuation.
Choices A, B, and C either use a comma alone, add a conjunction incorrectly, or insert a conjunctive adverb without the required semicolon, which fails to properly join the two independent clauses.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
